title: Ominous Steel
description: Adds new armors and tools obtained from Ominous Vaults.
body:
# **Discription**
This mod adds ominous scrap which can be found in Ominous Vaults. With the scrap you can craft an Ominous Steel Ingot which you can use to make a new Armor and Tool set.

## Items
You can craft the Ominous Upgrade Template like this:


<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

Normal:

![upgrade](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/2499574d3a1330c8a7e6453d7bb0b24db0df8253.png)

Duped:

![upgrade_dupe](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/9b4673f5d419c97308882ff590edf73ae58257b3.png)

</details>

You can then upgrade Netherite Armor and Tools using a Ominous Steel Ingot and a Ominous Template in a Smithing Table to get the Ominous Steel Armor set and the Ominous Steel Tools.


<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

![ominous_sword_upgrade](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/222e3a17d170732602b688369a8fb4bd1e2e8834.png)

</details>

### Ominous Steel Sword
The Ominous Steel Sword has the same attack as a Mace but is weaker when you fall from greater distances. You can activate this attack by falling 1 Block with the Sword. If you fall from 40 blocks or greater your fall distance will be divided by 10 and added as damage. The Knockback effect of the impact is also smaller than with the Mace. The Ominous Steel Sword can only be enchanted with sword enchantments and does not use Mace enchantments.

### Ominous Steel Armor
A full set of Ominous Steel Armor will grant the player a permantent Trial Omen V effect. The Armor also has more durability and protection than Netherite Armor but has less Knockback Resistance.
